title: 'Alien: Romulus' Ends 'Deadpool & Wolverine's' Three-Week Box Office Reign With $41.5 Million Debut
text: The seventh installment in the sci-fi horror franchise opened domestically with $41.5 million, dethroning Marvel's superhero adventure after three consecutive weekends atop the charts. Director Fede Alvarez's film achieved the second-highest debut in the "Alien" series history, behind only "Prometheus." The movie earned $108.2 million globally during its opening weekend. It garnered positive reception, holding an 82% freshness rating on Rotten Tomatoes and earning a "B+" CinemaScore from audiences. The film follows young colonists who encounter a terrifying creature while scavenging an abandoned space station. The production, which cost $80 million, stars Cailee Spaeny, David Jonsson, Archie Renaux, and Isabela Merced. Industry analyst David A. Gross noted: "This is an excellent opening for a film this deep into its series. In 1979, the first film elevated the quality of creature filmmaking." Meanwhile, "Deadpool & Wolverine" secured second place with $29 million in its fourth weekend, bringing its North American total to $545.8 million. The film recently surpassed "Joker" as the highest-grossing R-rated film ever.
